occurrence_sentence: University of Arizona Researchers Hone in on Quantum GPS, New Technologies

University of Arizona engineering and optical sciences researchers, in collaboration with engineers from General Dynamics Mission Systems, demonstrate how a combination of two techniques – radio frequency photonics sensing and quantum metrology – can give sensor networks a previously unheard-of level of precision.

University of Arizona engineering and optical sciences researchers, in collaboration with engineers from General Dynamics Mission Systems, demonstrate how a combination of two techniques – radio frequency photonics sensing and quantum metrology – can give sensor networks a previously unheard-of level of precision.

interaction_phrase: an assistant professor of [electrical and computer engineering]
occurrence_sentence: Quntao Zhuang, an assistant professor of [electrical and computer engineering](https://ece.engineering.arizona.edu/), [previously demonstrated](https://journals.aps.org/pra/abstract/10.1103/PhysRevA.97.032329) a theoretical framework to boost performance by teaming up entangled sensors.

interaction_phrase: being built on the University of Arizona campus
occurrence_sentence: One potential application is in the [entangled photon network](https://uanews.arizona.edu/story/interdisciplinary-ua-researchers-get-tangled-quantum-computing) being built on the University of Arizona campus.

interaction_phrase: the paper's authors were Yi Xia
occurrence_sentence: In addition to Zhang and Zhuang, the paper's authors were **Yi Xia** , a doctoral student in optical sciences; **Wei Li** , a postdoctoral researcher in the Department of Materials Science and Engineering; and William Clark and Darlene Hart of General Dynamics Mission Systems.
